item.title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language ModelSofroniew et al., 2026We find representations of emotion concepts in Claude Sonnet 4.5 and show that they causally influence its outputs.
item.description: s.

item.title: Circuits Cross-Post √¢¬Ä¬î Activation OraclesWe train language models to answer questions about their own activations in natural language.
Circuits Updates √¢¬Ä¬î November 2025A short update on harm pressure.

item.title: When Models Manipulate Manifolds: The Geometry of a Counting TaskGurnee et al., 2025We find geometric structure underlying the mechanisms of a fundamental language model behavior.
item.description: r.

item.title: Emergent Introspective Awareness in Large Language ModelsLindsey, 2025We find evidence that language models can introspect on their internal states.

item.title: A Toy Model of Mechanistic (Un)FaithfulnessWhen transcoders go awry.
item.description: . Tracing Attention Computation Through Feature Interactions Kamath et al., 2025 We describe and apply a method to explain attention patterns in terms of feature interactions, and integrate this information into attribution graphs. A Toy Model of Interference Weights Unpacking "interference weights" in some more depth. Sparse mixtures of linear transforms We investigate sparse mixture of linear transforms (MOLT), a new approach to transcoders. Circuits Updates √¢¬Ä¬î

item.title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language ModelsAmeisen et al., 2025We describe an approach to tracing the "step-by-step" computation involved when a model responds to a single prompt.
item.description: t.

item.title: On the Biology of a Large Language ModelLindsey et al., 2025We investigate the internal mechanisms used by Claude 3.5 Haiku √¢¬Ä¬î Anthropic's lightweight production model √¢¬Ä¬î in a variety of contexts.
item.description: s. Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models Ameisen et al., 2025 We describe an approach to tracing the "step-by-step" computation involved when a model responds to a single prompt.

item.title: Insights on Crosscoder Model DiffingA preliminary note on using crosscoders to diff models.
item.description: Insights on Crosscoder Model DiffingA preliminary note on using crosscoders to diff models.

item.title: Sparse mixtures of linear transformsWe investigate sparse mixture of linear transforms (MOLT), a new approach to transcoders.
item.description: Sparse mixtures of linear transformsWe investigate sparse mixture of linear transforms (MOLT), a new approach to transcoders.

item.title: Progress on AttentionAn update on our progress studying attention.
item.description: Progress on AttentionAn update on our progress studying attention.

item.title: Automated AuditingA note on using agents to perform automated alignment audits, including using interpretability tools.
item.description: Automated AuditingA note on using agents to perform automated alignment audits, including using interpretability tools.

item.title: Using Dictionary Learning Features as ClassifiersA preliminary note comparing feature-based and raw-activation based harmfulness classifiers.
Sparse Crosscoders for Cross-Layer Features and Model DiffingA preliminary note on a way to get consistent features across layers, and even models.
item.description: . Using Dictionary Learning Features as Classifiers A preliminary note comparing feature-based and raw-activation based harmfulness classifiers.

item.title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 SonnetTempleton et al., 2024Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a large number of interpretable features from Claude 3 Sonnet. Some appear to be safety-relevant.

item.title: Reflections on Qualitative ResearchSome opinionated thoughts on why interpretability research may have qualitative aspects be more central than we're used to in other fields.
item.description: Reflections on Qualitative ResearchSome opinionated thoughts on why interpretability research may have qualitative aspects be more central than we're used to in other fields.

item.title: Stage-Wise Model DiffingA preliminary note on model diffing through dictionary fine-tuning.
item.description: Stage-Wise Model DiffingA preliminary note on model diffing through dictionary fine-tuning.

item.title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary LearningBricken et al., 2023Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a large number of interpretable features from a one-layer transformer.
item.description: r.

item.title: Circuits Updates √¢¬Ä¬î July 2023A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.
item.description: Circuits Updates √¢¬Ä¬î July 2023A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Distributed Representations: Composition & SuperpositionAn informal note on how "distributed representations" might be understood as two different, competing strategies √¢¬Ä¬î "composition" and "superposition" √¢¬Ä¬î with quite different properties.
Circuits Updates √¢¬Ä¬î May 2023A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.
item.description: Circuits Updates √¢¬Ä¬î May 2023A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Privileged Bases in the Transformer Residual StreamOur mathematical theories of the Transformer architecture suggest that individual coordinates in the residual stream should have no special significance, but recent work has shown that this observation is false in practice. We investigate this phenomenon and provisionally conclude that the per-dimension normalizers in the Adam optimizer are to blame for the effect.

Superposition, Memorization, and Double DescentHenighan et al., 2023We have little mechanistic understanding of how deep learning models overfit to their training data, despite it being a central problem. Here we extend our previous work on toy models to shed light on how models generalize beyond their training data.
item.description: a.

item.title: Interpretability DreamsOur present research aims to create a foundation for mechanistic interpretability research. In doing so, it's important to keep sight of what we're trying to lay the foundations for.
item.description: Interpretability DreamsOur present research aims to create a foundation for mechanistic interpretability research. In doing so, it's important to keep sight of what we're trying to lay the foundations for.

item.title: Mechanistic Interpretability, Variables, and the Importance of Interpretable BasesAn informal note on intuitions related to mechanistic interpretability.

Toy Models of SuperpositionElhage et al., 2022Neural networks often seem to pack many unrelated concepts into a single neuron - a puzzling phenomenon known as 'polysemanticity'. In our latest interpretability work, we build toy models where the origins and dynamics of polysemanticity can be fully understood.
item.description: Toy Models of SuperpositionElhage et al., 2022Neural networks often seem to pack many unrelated concepts into a single neuron - a puzzling phenomenon known as 'polysemanticity'. In our latest interpretability work, we build toy models where the origins and dynamics of polysemanticity can be fully understood.

item.title: Softmax Linear UnitsAn alternative activation function increases the fraction of neurons which appear to correspond to human-understandable concepts.
item.description: Softmax Linear UnitsAn alternative activation function increases the fraction of neurons which appear to correspond to human-understandable concepts.

item.title: In-Context Learning and Induction HeadsOlsson et al., 2022An exploration of the hypothesis that induction heads are the primary mechanism behind in-context learning. We also report the existence of a previously unknown phase change in transformers language models.paper
item.description: In-Context Learning and Induction HeadsOlsson et al., 2022An exploration of the hypothesis that induction heads are the primary mechanism behind in-context learning. We also report the existence of a previously unknown phase change in transformers language models.paper

item.title: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer CircuitsElhage et al., 2021Our early mathematical framework for reverse engineering models, demonstrated by reverse engineering small toy models.paper
item.description: per Exercises Some exercises we've developed to improve our understanding of how neural networks implement algorithms at the parameter level. note, exercises Videos Very rough informal talks as we search for a way to reverse engineering transformers. links, videos PySvelte One approach to bridging Python and web-based interactive diagrams for interpretability research. github link, infrastructure Garcon A description of our tooling for

item.title: VideosVery rough informal talks as we search for a way to reverse engineering transformers.links, videos

item.title: GarconA description of our tooling for doing interpretability on large models.note, infrastructure
item.description: GarconA description of our tooling for doing interpretability on large models.note, infrastructure

item.title: ExercisesSome exercises we've developed to improve our understanding of how neural networks implement algorithms at the parameter level.note, exercises

item.title: Distill on Hiatus
item.description: , we're taking a page from David Ha√¢¬Ä¬ôs approach of simply creating websites (eg. World Models ) for research projects.

item.title: Original Distill Circuits ThreadOur exploration of Transformers builds heavily on the original Circuits thread on Distill.
item.description: Original Distill Circuits ThreadOur exploration of Transformers builds heavily on the original Circuits thread on Distill.

item.title: Activation Atlases
item.description: for a striking example). Previously we would have submitted to Distill, but with Distill on Hiatus , we're taking a page from David Ha√¢¬Ä¬ôs approach of simply creating websites (eg. World Models ) for research projects.
